title: 2 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Just Got Kicked Out of the S&P 500. Is Either a Buy Now?
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: Campbell's Company and Pool Corporation were removed from the S&P 500 on June 22, triggering mechanical selling pressure. Campbell's offers a rare 7%+ dividend yield backed by 51 years of payments and the growing Rao's brand, though dividend growth has been slow. Pool Corp. features a more modest 2.4% yield but has raised dividends for 22 consecutive years at ~17% annually, supported by recurring maintenance revenue and digital platform growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite recent operational headwinds and slow dividend growth, the stock offers an attractive 7%+ yield backed by 51 years of consistent dividend payments, strong cash flow coverage, and the promising Rao's brand which crossed $1 billion in sales. The removal creates a temporary buying opportunity.

Demonstrates exceptional dividend growth with 22 consecutive years of increases at ~17% annually, supported by recurring maintenance revenue (60% of sales), solid earnings growth (Q1 2026: 6% sales, 7% operating income growth), and emerging digital platform growth. Strong execution of dividend growth strategy despite housing market sensitivity.

title: Pool Corporation Announces Increase in Share Repurchase Program, Growth of Quarterly Dividend and Results of 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders
author: Na
description: Pool Corporation announced an increase in its share repurchase program to $600 million (adding $329 million to the existing program) and raised its quarterly dividend by 4% to $1.30 per share. The company also held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, electing nine directors and ratifying Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company demonstrated strong capital allocation discipline by increasing share repurchases by $329 million and raising the quarterly dividend by 4%, signaling management confidence in future cash generation and commitment to returning value to shareholders.

title: 2 Oversold Dividend Growth Stocks to Buy Now
author: Daniel Sparks
description: Moody's and Pool Corp have experienced significant stock price declines (16% and 11% respectively year-to-date) despite strong fundamentals. Moody's reported 13% revenue growth and 39% earnings growth in Q4 2025, while Pool Corp faces cyclical headwinds but maintains a secure dividend with a 2.4% yield. Both companies have consistent dividend growth track records and are considered buying opportunities for long-term investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q4 2025 results with 13% revenue growth and 39% earnings per share growth. Recently raised dividend by 10% for 17th consecutive year. Conservative payout ratio of 29% provides room for future increases. Stock decline to P/E of 31 is considered reasonable for a high-margin compounder.

Despite cyclical headwinds and declining Q4 earnings, the company maintains a secure dividend with 2.4% yield and 45% payout ratio. 15-year streak of consecutive dividend increases. Non-discretionary maintenance products provide earnings floor. Stock trading at attractive P/E of 19 based on cyclically depressed earnings, positioning it as a turnaround play.

title: Pool Stock Is Down 35% This Past Year, and One Fund Recently Disclosed Dumping a $10 Million Stake
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Wedgewood Partners exited its entire $10 million stake in Pool Corporation (POOL) during Q4 2025. Pool's stock has declined 35% over the past year as the company navigates a normalization phase following pandemic-era demand. Despite solid fundamentals with $5.3B in 2025 revenue and steady 29.7% gross margins, the fund's exit reflects a preference for secular tech compounders over cyclical distributors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock down 35% over the past year, major fund (Wedgewood Partners) completely exited its position, diluted EPS fell 4% YoY, operating income declined, and the company is in a normalization phase after pandemic-driven growth pull-forward. While fundamentals aren't collapsing, the trajectory and institutional selling signal weakness.
Listed as Wedgewood Partners' top holding at $56.12M (10.5% of AUM) after the Pool exit, indicating strong confidence in this mega-cap technology secular compounder.
Second-largest holding in Wedgewood Partners at $51.30M (9.6% of AUM), representing the fund's preference for secular tech compounders over cyclical businesses.
Second-largest holding in Wedgewood Partners at $51.30M (9.6% of AUM), representing the fund's preference for secular tech compounders over cyclical businesses.
Third-largest holding in Wedgewood Partners at $45.64M (8.5% of AUM), consistent with the fund's strategy of favoring mega-cap technology names.
Fourth-largest holding in Wedgewood Partners at $38.85M (7.3% of AUM), reflecting confidence in this secular technology compounder.
Fifth-largest holding in Wedgewood Partners at $37.48M (7.0% of AUM), part of the fund's core mega-cap tech portfolio strategy.

title: Pool Corporation Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
author: Globe Newswire
description: Pool Corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $1.25 per share, payable on March 26, 2026 to shareholders of record on March 12, 2026. The world's largest wholesale distributor of swimming pool and related backyard products operates approximately 455 sales centers across North America, Europe, and Australia.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a quarterly cash dividend of $1.25 per share demonstrates the company's financial health and confidence in its business performance. Regular dividend payments are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate stable cash flows and shareholder-friendly capital allocation.
